text:
Athletes hoping to feast on chicken nuggets before or after competing in their event at the 2024 Paris Olympics are set to be disappointed, with a senior official confirming the popular snack would not be feature on the menu.

Competitors looking forward to dining on chicken nuggets at this year’s Olympics in Paris are about to be sorely disappointed amid confirmation the product won't feature on the official menu.

The Games’ head of food and beverages Philipp Würz toldThe Telegraphthe popular item would not be available, although plant-based alternatives are on offer.

“Chicken nuggets we do not offer,” he said.

However, he said the chefs were aware some athletes like to eat fast food after they finish their events, revealing freshly grilled steaks would be delivered through the main kitchen.

“20 per cent of the total number of meals that had to be delivered for the London Games came out of the McDonald’s outlet,” he told the masthead.

“So this actually already shows you why junk food or burger food is important to the athletes.”

McDonalds officially sponsored the Olympic Games for 41 years, but the fast food chain ended the deal in 2017, according to Reuters.

Legendary sprinter Usain Bolt reportedly ate about 100 Chicken McNuggets a day while he was in Beijing, China for the 2008 Olympics Games.

Mr Würz said this year’s event is all about showcasing French cuisine while catering to the specific requests of athletes.

Many competitors are already arriving at the Olympic village in Paris, with videos posted on social media giving the public an inside look at the accommodation.

Aussie tennis player Daria Saville shared a TikTok showing a “club” outdoor space with a DJ, beanbag chairs and non-alcoholic drinks.

There’s also a custom coffee station, a bakery, fitness centre, training grounds, dining hall, restaurants and prayer rooms.

The Village spans across Saint-Denis, Saint Ouen and L'Île-Saint-Denis and will accommodate 14,250 athletes during the Olympic Games, and 8,000 during the Paralympic Games, according to the official website.

All the action officially begins on Friday, July 26.
